old_claim.note: Glassmaking provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Al-Kindi's documented apparatus uses a glass receiver and warns about glass breakage; glassmaking therefore enabled a specific apparatus component without being the only possible vessel technology.

source_shape.source_locator: pp. 88-89 specify a glass receiver at the alembic delivery tube and warn that temperature shock can break the glass apparatus.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The ninth-century procedure directly identifies a glass receiving vessel as part of the distillation unit.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Glassmaking is enabling rather than required because the source documents a concrete glass component while the rest of the apparatus also uses pottery and wood.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-medieval-alembic-distillation-scope: glassmaking remains an enabling material route with apparatus-specific evidence.
would_reject_if: The cited receiver is reinterpreted as non-glass.
The node is narrowed to an all-ceramic apparatus that excludes glass components.
A dedicated laboratory_glassware node is added and should replace broad glassmaking.
short_reason: Al-Kindi's apparatus explicitly includes a glass receiver.
